summary: qontract-reconcile dev release substitutes httpx with httpxyz typosquat across imports and uv resolver overrides
details: This dev release of qontract-reconcile replaces the legitimate `httpx` HTTP client with `httpxyz`, a typosquat of `httpx` not controlled by the package's stated publisher. Multiple modules unconditionally `import httpxyz` at module top-level, including `reconcile/utils/runtime/integration.py` (line 18) and `reconcile/ldap_users_api/integration.py` (line 14). A residual comment in `reconcile/utils/runtime/environment.py` line 67 (`# hide logging.info "HTTP GET/POST..." logs from httpxyz`) confirms a global httpx→httpxyz textual substitution. The `reconcile/cli.py` console-script entrypoint imports the affected runtime module, so any invocation of the `qontract-reconcile` CLI loads whatever `httpxyz` resolves to on PyPI. To force resolution of the typosquat into the environment, `pyproject.toml` lines 104-106 declare a `[[tool.uv.dependency-metadata]]` override that rewrites the legitimate `pagerduty` SDK's published dependency from `httpx` to `httpxyz>=0.31`, ensuring `uv`-based installs pull the attacker-controlled package transitively even where it is not listed directly. The combination of (a) module-level imports of a typosquat, (b) resolver-metadata override coercing a legitimate transitive into the typosquat, and (c) exposure via the CLI entrypoint operationalizes a dependency-confusion attack on installers/operators of this package: arbitrary code published under the `httpxyz` name on PyPI runs at CLI startup.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
